---
title: "Basic T Demo: MTCars Analysis"
format: html
filters:
  - tlang
---

# Introduction

This demo showcases a basic data analysis pipeline in the **T Programming Language**.
Each chunk below is executed by the T interpreter during the Quarto rendering process.

# Data Inspection

We can access pipeline nodes directly. In Quarto, `read_node("name")` provides metadata about the node, including its path.

```{t}
#| label: load-data
# Read the node metadata
mt_node = read_node("mtcars")

# Actually read the data from its artifact path
df = read_csv(mt_node.path)
df |> glimpse
```

# Filtering Results

The pipeline also produced a filtered version of the dataset.

```{t}
#| label: filter-data
filtered_node = read_node("filtered_mtcars")
filtered = read_csv(filtered_node.path)

print(str_join(["Number of rows in filtered data: ", nrow(filtered)]))
print(str_nchar("Hello world"))
head(filtered, 5)
```

# Accessing Artifact Paths

The `path` attribute allows for easy integration with any tool that expects a file.

```{t}
#| label: path-demo
m_node = read_node("mtcars_mpg")
print(str_join(["MPG artifact path: ", m_node.path]))
```

# Conclusion

The T language orchestrates the entire workflow, and Quarto provides a beautiful way to document and share the results, all within the same ecosystem.
